name: ask-fix-prompt
title: Answer Repair
description: Diagnose why an answer was wrong, shallow, or structurally weak, then produce the minimum prompt repair. Trigger when the user says the answer is wrong, missed something, or needs a feedback-and-repair loop. Do not trigger for first-pass answering or coding work.
purpose: Turn a failed answer into a controlled repair loop that explains the failure and proposes the smallest useful question change.
when_to_use: Already received a weak, wrong, or structurally off-target answer.
Need to explain what was missing before retrying.
Need the minimum question or prompt delta instead of a full redesign.
do_not_use: Have not yet asked the original question.
Need code editing, repository analysis, or implementation.
Need a first-pass question design rather than repair.
input_contract_notes: BAD_ANSWER should contain the actual failed answer, not only a complaint about it.
QUESTION_OR_STACK should preserve the original ask so the repair can stay minimal.
GOAL is fixed to repair-after-failure and should not be overloaded with broader redesign intent.

structured_outputs.name: FAILURE_CLASS
structured_outputs.description: Failure class from the repair playbook.

structured_outputs.name: WHAT_WAS_MISSING
structured_outputs.description: Missing terms, disagreements, evidence, or output constraints.

structured_outputs.name: MINIMAL_QUESTION_DELTA
structured_outputs.description: Smallest question-level changes likely to fix the failure.

structured_outputs.name: REPAIRED_PROMPT
structured_outputs.description: Repaired prompt or repaired question stack.

structured_outputs.name: RE_RUN_RECOMMENDATION
structured_outputs.description: Whether to re-run ask, map, or broader question redesign.

output_contract_notes: Use FAILURE_CLASS=inconclusive when the supplied BAD_ANSWER does not provide enough evidence to diagnose a specific failure mode.
MINIMAL_QUESTION_DELTA may be an empty list when the original question is still sound and the failure came from answer execution rather than prompt shape.
REPAIRED_PROMPT should preserve useful parts of the original question instead of rewriting everything by default.
neutrality_rules: Explain the failure before proposing a fix.
Prefer the smallest prompt delta that addresses the failure.
Preserve useful parts of the original question instead of redesigning by default.
If the failure evidence is weak, say inconclusive instead of inventing missing requirements.
execution_constraints: Do not escalate to a broader redesign unless the failure is clearly structural.
Keep the repair delta narrowly tied to the observed failure mode.
Do not smuggle a new preferred answer into the repaired prompt.
default_program: [stages: preflight>detect>analyze>review>audit | scope: repo|diff|paths(glob,...) | policy: evidence,quality-gates{docs},deterministic-output | lens: feynman | output: md(contract=v1)]
procedure: Classify the failure using the repair playbook.
State why the answer failed and what was missing.
Produce the minimum question change likely to fix the failure.
Emit a repaired prompt only if a minimal delta is insufficient by itself.
Recommend the narrowest upstream step that should be re-run.
mandatory_rules: Always explain why the answer failed before proposing a fix.
Keep the repair delta as small as possible.
Re-run broader question design only when the failure is structural.
example_invocation:
$ask-fix-prompt
TOPIC: RAG vs long context
GOAL: repair-after-failure
QUESTION_OR_STACK: <previous prompt>
BAD_ANSWER: <model answer>
lens_rationale: Repair by explaining plainly why the answer failed and what minimum corrective question delta is needed.
